solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int numSub(String s) {
        int mod = 1000000007;
        int count = 0;
        int consecutiveOnes = 0;

        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
            if (s.charAt(i) == '1') {
                consecutiveOnes++;
                count = (count + consecutiveOnes) % mod;
            } else {
                consecutiveOnes = 0;
            }
        }

        return count;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution iterates through the string once, tracking consecutive '1's and adding them to the count. For a group of k consecutive '1's, there are k + (k-1) + ... + 1 = k*(k+1)/2 substrings. This approach avoids recalculating and is efficient for large inputs.>
